decisions.issue: Database choice: PostgreSQL vs Firestore for transactional data
decisions.recommendation: Firestore
decisions.reasoning: Code already uses Firestore for all 13 collections (RBAC, audit, purchases, config). ARC-FINAL said PostgreSQL but code diverged. Migrating to PostgreSQL would rewrite 80% of backend. Firestore works, is in production-like state, and meets requirements for ~20 users.
alternatives: PostgreSQL (per ARC-FINAL — rejected: would require full rewrite)
Firestore (current — accepted: works, no migration needed)

decisions.issue: Warsaw BigQuery integration: cross-project reads vs own temp_invoice table
decisions.recommendation: Own temp_invoice table in BQ
decisions.reasoning: No cross-project reads configured or planned. We have our own temp_invoice table in BigQuery dataset itrp_warsaw_export. Data is loaded separately (mechanism TBD).

decisions.issue: PG→BQ nightly sync: needed or not?
decisions.recommendation: Not needed — dual-write at purchase time
decisions.reasoning: With Firestore as transactional store, purchase records are written to both Firestore AND BigQuery at purchase time (existing code does this). No nightly sync needed. Reporting reads directly from BQ tables populated at purchase.

decisions.issue: Credit limit display in customer detail — field exists in schema but always None
decisions.recommendation: DEFERRED until Warsaw credit limit sync is implemented
decisions.reasoning: credit_limit field in CustomerDetail schema is ready. Needs per-customer credit limits from Warsaw (BIZ-001 in DISCREPANCIES.md). Cannot implement without external data source.
decisions.resolution_notes: customers_master collection now has credit_limit per customer. Wire customer detail endpoint to read from it.

decisions.issue: Manually excluded debtors not yet reflected on ITRP main page eligibles
decisions.recommendation: DEFERRED — separate task needed to integrate customer_exclusions into buying service eligibility rules
decisions.reasoning: Exclusions are stored in Firestore (customer_exclusions collection) but buying service step 2 eligibility rules do not check this collection. Needs a new eligibility rule that cross-references customer_exclusions before including invoices.
decisions.resolution_notes: Implement Rule 8 in buying service step_2 — check customer_exclusions before including invoices.

decisions.issue: Reporting does not filter out excluded debtors
decisions.recommendation: DEFERRED — reporting page not yet implemented
decisions.reasoning: Reporting module is a separate feature (GAP-005 in DISCREPANCIES.md). When implemented, report queries must exclude invoices from customers in customer_exclusions collection.

decisions.issue: TASK-201 and TASK-202 (Warsaw Cloud Storage + Data Pipeline) excluded - no Warsaw access
decisions.recommendation: OUT_OF_SCOPE for current phase. Mock data via seed scripts instead.

decisions.issue: TASK-605 BigQuery Materialized Views marked SUPERSEDED
decisions.recommendation: Dual-write at purchase time already in buying service steps 6-7. No nightly sync needed.
decisions.reasoning: Verified in buying/service.py - step_6_persist_job_record and step_7_update_reporting both write to BQ tables directly.

decisions.issue: Nav Bar (TASK-304) not a separate objective - included in O-005 Dashboard as first task
decisions.recommendation: Nav Bar is app shell - build as part of Dashboard objective since Dashboard is the landing page

decisions.issue: TASK-506 My Customers already implemented in T-005 through T-012
decisions.recommendation: Skip - already done with 5 backend endpoints + frontend page + country scoping + exclusions + sales type override

decisions.issue: Separate legal_entities collection vs using opcos collection for legal entity data
decisions.recommendation: Keep legal entity data on opcos collection. No separate collection needed.
decisions.reasoning: 1. buying/service.py uses opco_map keyed by legal_entity_code — creating separate collection breaks this. 2. OpCo code IS the legal entity code in BQ temp_invoice data. 3. project.md says 1 OpCo can have many Legal Entities but current data treats them as 1:1. 4. Separate collection adds complexity (joins across collections) without business value for current ~75 OpCo dataset.
alternatives: Create separate legal_entities collection with FK to OpCo (rejected: breaks buying service, adds Firestore join complexity)
Keep on opcos (chosen: simpler, matches current code, no refactor needed)

decisions.issue: Multi-currency auto-buy schedule vs single schedule per country
decisions.recommendation: Keep single schedule per country. No per-currency split.
decisions.reasoning: Each ITRP country has one primary currency. opco_schedules stores one record per country which is sufficient. Multi-currency UI adds complexity without business value for 11 countries. Can extend later with currency field if needed.
alternatives: Single per-country (chosen: matches current data model)

decisions.issue: DEFERRED: File last loaded real data
decisions.recommendation: No data pipeline — mock status until Warsaw integration
decisions.reasoning: Identified during completion of T-037. Source requirement not implemented in this task.

decisions.issue: DEFERRED: Real cashflow from rpt_daily_estimation
decisions.recommendation: BQ table not created yet
decisions.reasoning: Identified during completion of T-043. Source requirement not implemented in this task.

decisions.issue: DEFERRED: Manual buy functionality
decisions.recommendation: Buying service in O-007
decisions.reasoning: Identified during completion of T-043. Source requirement not implemented in this task.

decisions.issue: OI-01: ITRP Manager maintenance:write conflicts with RBAC matrix (Read). Spec docs say ITRP Manager must toggle auto-buy, set start dates, re-enable after restore.
decisions.recommendation: Keep maintenance:write for ITRP Manager. Consider splitting into granular permissions (schedule:write vs restore:execute) in future.
decisions.reasoning: Without write, ITRP Manager cannot perform daily operations described in AUTO-BUY-analysis and SPEC docs. Matryca is outdated vs operational reality.

decisions.issue: OI-06: Back Office country scoping — global or assigned countries?
decisions.recommendation: Implement country scoping (Option B) for Back Office, same as ITRP Manager. Currently global in seed.
decisions.reasoning: COUNTRY-ACCESS-SCOPING recommends Option B for both roles. Safer default. Easy to override with all-countries assignment.

decisions.issue: OI-07: Auto-provisioning of new users on first SSO login
decisions.recommendation: Defer. Mock SSO + manual user creation in Admin panel is sufficient for now.
decisions.reasoning: Meeting notes say SSO will be implemented later. For now mockup. No blocker.

decisions.issue: OI-P01: Default priority order when no manual priority set
decisions.recommendation: document_date ASC, open_amount DESC — already implemented in Step 3 buying service
decisions.reasoning: Sensible default: older invoices first, larger amounts as tiebreaker. Already working in code.

decisions.issue: OI-P06: Priority data model — Option A (field) vs Option B (separate collection)
decisions.recommendation: Option B (separate invoice_priorities collection) — already implemented
decisions.reasoning: Clean separation, natural audit trail with set_by/set_at. Already in production code.

decisions.issue: OI-13: Credit limit breach timeout — what if nobody responds?
decisions.recommendation: Defer. Requires FTC decision on timeout behavior.
decisions.reasoning: Auto-buy pauses on breach. Timeout behavior undefined. Not blocking current implementation phase.

decisions.issue: OI-01: ITRP Manager RBAC on schedule maintenance - read-only or write?
decisions.recommendation: ITRP Manager gets maintenance:write for assigned countries
decisions.reasoning: Documentation has conflict: RBAC matrix says Read-only, User Interactions table says write. User decided: ITRP Manager should have write access to schedule for their assigned countries. Country-scoping enforced server-side via user_country_assignments.
alternatives: Read-only per RBAC matrix - rejected: too restrictive
Write for assigned countries - accepted: matches operational need

decisions.issue: OI-04/OI-S02: Exact Cloud Scheduler cron time — must fire after Warsaw file delivery window closes. What time does Warsaw deposit data?
decisions.recommendation: Default 06:00 UTC. Adjust when Warsaw confirms delivery window.
decisions.reasoning: No document specifies Warsaw delivery time. 06:00 UTC is current default in Terraform. Severity if wrong: MEDIUM — auto-buy may run against incomplete data.
decisions.override_value: 06:00 UTC default. Infrastructure-level decision — not blocking app code.
decisions.override_reason: Cron time is infra config, not app logic. App code uses Trigger Service which is time-agnostic. Adjustable in Terraform without code changes.

decisions.issue: OI-05/OI-S03: File-completion gate mechanism — how does Trigger Service know data is loaded before running auto-buy?
decisions.recommendation: Use database flag (file_load_status table) set by data pipeline on successful load. Trigger Service polls this flag before initiating.
decisions.reasoning: Three options: DB flag, Cloud Storage event, polling. DB flag is simplest and testable. Mockup stubs exist in code already.
decisions.override_value: Database flag approach: file_load_status collection in Firestore. Data pipeline sets flag on completion. Trigger Service checks before proceeding.
decisions.override_reason: Simplest testable approach. Mockup stubs already exist. Can evolve to event-driven later without API changes.

decisions.issue: OI-06/OI-S08: Week boundary definition — what constitutes current week for latest file?
decisions.recommendation: Monday 00:00 UTC to Sunday 23:59 UTC (ISO week). Same for all countries.
decisions.reasoning: Not formally specified. UTC avoids timezone complexity. Country-local time would add significant complexity for 12 timezone configs.
decisions.override_value: Monday 00:00 UTC to Sunday 23:59 UTC (ISO week). Same for all countries.
decisions.override_reason: UTC avoids timezone complexity. Consistent with existing code (Step 1 uses UTC hardcoded). Country-local time can be added later if needed.

decisions.issue: Step 0 pre-buy validation: exact rules and failure behavior undefined
decisions.recommendation: Implement known candidates (non-null invoice_id, valid date, positive amount, matching currency, existing legal_entity, existing customer). Fail entire job on validation failure (consistent with all-or-nothing model).
decisions.reasoning: AUTO-BUY-analysis.md states exact checks TBD in separate story. Known candidates listed. Failure behavior options: fail job or exclude invoice. Recommend fail-job for consistency with Step 5 rollback model.
decisions.override_value: Implement known candidates. On validation failure: exclude invalid invoices and log, do not fail entire job (different from Step 5 all-or-nothing which is about purchase failures, not data quality).
decisions.override_reason: Step 0 is data quality, Step 5 is purchase execution. Data quality issues = exclude and continue. Purchase failures = rollback all. Different severity levels.

decisions.issue: Credit limit breach — concurrent country behavior
decisions.recommendation: Pause only the affected country job. Other countries continue independently.
decisions.reasoning: Not defined in source docs. Per-country independence is the general architectural principle (each country has own lock, own schedule). Pausing globally would delay all countries for one customer issue.
decisions.override_value: Pause only the affected country job. Other countries continue independently.
decisions.override_reason: Per-country independence is core architecture. Each country has own lock, own schedule, own Cloud Scheduler trigger.

decisions.issue: Email notification service — which GCP service to use?
decisions.recommendation: Evaluate: (1) SendGrid (GCP Marketplace), (2) Cloud Tasks + SMTP relay, (3) Pub/Sub + Cloud Function + SMTP. Stub implementation first, wire real service later.
decisions.reasoning: User confirmed no email service exists yet. GCP does not have native email sending. Need to pick a service. Start with stub.
decisions.override_value: Start with stub email service (logs to console/file). Evaluate SendGrid vs Cloud Tasks+SMTP when GCP email requirements are clarified. Interface: NotificationService.send_email(template, recipients, data).
decisions.override_reason: No email service selected yet. Stub-first allows full pipeline testing. Clean interface enables swap later.

decisions.issue: Warsaw API integration for Step 5 purchase execution
decisions.recommendation: Build as stub (write records to BQ without calling external API). Wire real Warsaw API when spec available.
decisions.reasoning: User confirmed no Warsaw API spec available. Cannot implement real integration. Stub allows full pipeline testing.
